mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.0% threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deltas in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20-day returns with greater than 55% accuracy over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation above 260 combined with a low federal funds rate forces anticipated aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260 and FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO deltas correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in total nonfarm payrolls below 138,000 thousand signals severe labor market contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS falls below the 138,000 threshold over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% alongside CPIAUCSL growth below 3% annually confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of strong GDPC1 growth and contained CPIAUCSL fails to predict positive 60-day returns with greater than 50% accuracy over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment exceeds 6.0% while industrial production declines, the economy enters a stagflationary trap that forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0% and INDPRO shows negative month-over-month growth.
